paragraphs: LAUNCH CONFIG
BIBLE MADE SIMPLE - POV/UGC BATCH MASTER LAUNCH FILE

Everything in one place: video file → ad set → naming string → ad copy. All 10 ads (026–035), decoded directly from the actual video files. V2: every ad copy opens with the video's on-screen POV hook block verbatim, and the body uses only lines the VO/captions actually say (light connective tissue only, no invented scenes or payoffs).
QUICK REFERENCE TABLE

Ad-set settings: Broad · 25–54 W (covers Hannah 26–42, Grace 30–48, Rebecca 34–52) · ADV+ all placements · ~$15/day/ad set · $0 Free+Fee offer · peacefulpath.store PDP. Judge on the AOV ≥ $5 custom conversion, never the raw free ATC event.

Naming notes: Rows 026–035 assume long-form Batch 1 holds rows 001–025; confirm against the DNA tracker before pasting. Date placeholder 7-14-2026, change to real launch day. All audio tagged a:UGC voiceover per the locked brief; confirm by listening (see VERIFICATION & FLAGS).
AD SET 1 - CREATOR POV (videos 1-3)

Creator on camera through the first ~15–20s, POV hook block in second person, VO flips to first person on the same facts (locked sync mechanic confirmed on all three). Book-flip reveal mid-video. This is the closest match to the reference winner formula. Feed it first.
AD 026 - BMS_Video_-_1.mp4

NAME: DA | 7-14-2026 | ACQ | BMS POV-UGC Video ADs | Row 26 | Round-1 | f:UGC | af:CreatorPOV+BookReveal | m:POV-Restart-AGAIN | h:POV Started Over With God Four Times This Year | p:Bible-Made-Simple-71p-Guide | i:none | len:39sec | o:Free+Fee | lp:pdp | a:UGC voiceover | V1 | Shop Now
AD COPY:

POV: you've started over with God four times this year. you downloaded three Bible apps. you bought the journal with the gold edges. you still close it after ten minutes, more lost than when you opened it.
why does everyone else get it but me 🥲
that was me. and i still closed it, every time, more lost than when i opened it.
i wasn't backsliding. i kept coming back. that was the whole problem.
i thought i needed more discipline. i needed someone to explain it. in normal words. about who's talking. why it matters.
that's what this 71 page guide is. the whole Bible in plain English, illustrated, no 15-tab study setup.
i read the first section and understood more of it than i ever had.
it's FREE today only. tomorrow it goes back up to $79.99.
👉 tap below before midnight.
AD 027 - BMS_Video_-_2.mp4

NAME: DA | 7-14-2026 | ACQ | BMS POV-UGC Video ADs | Row 27 | Round-1 | f:UGC | af:CreatorPOV+BookReveal | m:POV-HomeschoolFear-FEAR | h:POV Teach Your Kids Scripture Terrified Of A Question You Cant Answer | p:Bible-Made-Simple-71p-Guide | i:none | len:45sec | o:Free+Fee | lp:pdp | a:UGC voiceover | V1 | Shop Now
AD COPY:
POV: you teach your kids scripture every morning. you lead the homeschool devotional. and quietly, you're terrified someone will ask a question you can't answer.
teaching what i don't fully get 😬
the famous stories i could do. Noah, David, the Easter week. it was the prophets, the letters, the books in the middle. i was guessing.
i didn't want a commentary the size of a phone book. i wanted the actual map. what each book is, in order, in words a tired mom can hold.
that's what this 71 page guide is. the whole Bible, plain English, illustrated, book by book.
it changed our morning time. i'm teaching it now instead of surviving it.
my nine year old asked me about the prophets last week.
it's FREE today only. tomorrow it's $79.99.
👉 tap below before midnight.
AD 028 - BMS_Video_-_3.mp4

NAME: DA | 7-14-2026 | ACQ | BMS POV-UGC Video ADs | Row 28 | Round-1 | f:UGC | af:CreatorPOV+BookReveal | m:POV-GroupFaking-LANG | h:POV Small Group Nods Like The Verse Made Sense You Nod Too | p:Bible-Made-Simple-71p-Guide | i:none | len:39sec | o:Free+Fee | lp:pdp | a:UGC voiceover | V1 | Shop Now
AD COPY:
POV: everyone in your small group nods like the verse made perfect sense. you nod too. you have no idea what was just said. you've been faking it for two years.
smiling and lost 🙃
it wasn't that i didn't care. the words just didn't land. the thees and thous. the long list of names. the chapters that read like legal documents.
i thought that meant something was wrong with my faith. it didn't. nobody had ever just explained it plainly.
that's what this guide does. it takes the whole Bible and puts it in everyday language. every book, who it's for, why it's there. 71 pages, illustrated, no churchy vocabulary.
first time in two years, that wasn't a fake nod.
it's FREE today only. tomorrow it's $79.99.
👉 tap below before midnight.

Faceless book/nature B-roll carrying the deepest clusters: mom-gift guilt, fear of failing God, hidden shame. The heaviest emotional swings in the batch; watch comment sentiment closely in week one.
AD 029 - BMS_Video_-_4.mp4

AD COPY:
POV: your mom gave you a Bible when you left home. it's on the shelf, spine uncracked, eight years later. you feel the guilt every time you walk past it.
that was mine. and i felt the guilt every single time i walked past it.
it wasn't rebellion. every time i tried, i opened it, hit the wall of names, and put it back on the shelf.
what changed it was a guide that explained the book. what it's about. why it matters. 71 pages, plain English, illustrated.
and it wasn't a wall anymore.
i cracked open my mom's Bible last month. i called her after. she cried.
it's FREE today only. tomorrow it goes back up to $79.99.
👉 tap below before midnight.
AD 030 - BMS_Video_-_5.mp4

NAME: DA | 7-14-2026 | ACQ | BMS POV-UGC Video ADs | Row 30 | Round-1 | f:UGC | af:Faceless+BookBroll | m:Fear-FailingGod-FEAR | h:POV You Want To Believe But Failing At God Too Scares You | p:Bible-Made-Simple-71p-Guide | i:none | len:38sec | o:Free+Fee | lp:pdp | a:UGC voiceover | V1 | Shop Now
AD COPY:
POV: you want to believe. you really do. you've just quit so many things that starting again, and failing at God too, scares you more than not starting at all.
that was me. failing at God too scared me more than not starting at all.
and the fear was never God. the fear was opening that book, not understanding it, and taking it as proof i wasn't cut out for this.
so i started somewhere gentler. a 71 page guide. plain English. one short section at a time. it tells you what a book is about before you ever open it.
and something i didn't think was possible happened. it made sense.
it's FREE today only. tomorrow it goes back up to $79.99.

POV: you lead the morning devotional, but you secretly skip the books you don't understand. the prophets. the letters. half the old testament. you hope nobody notices.
the easy stories i could do. it was the hard middle that exposed me. the parts nobody ever explained to me. so i skipped them and hoped nobody noticed.
this guide gives you the whole Bible. every book, in order, plain. the scary ones included.
each one is short enough to read before the kids are awake. and now the kids follow along, instead of me squirming.
71 pages. FREE today only. tomorrow it's $79.99.

and it was never a faith problem. it was the on-ramp. nobody tells you where to begin, so you open to Genesis and grind.

LAUNCH SEQUENCE
Don't run all 10 at $15/day at once. Spend splinters and no ad gets enough data to read.
WEEK 1 - prove the angle (6 ads live):

AS1 Creator-POV: turn on Video 1 (026) + Video 3 (028). AS2 Faceless-Heavy: turn on Video 4 (029) + Video 5 (030). AS3 Faceless-Restart: turn on Video 8 (033) + Video 10 (035). Run each ad set toward ~50 purchases. Judge on cost-per-purchase on the AOV ≥ $5 conversion, not clicks and not free ATCs.
WEEK 2 - feed the winner:

Lowest cost-per-purchase ad set (bet: AS1 Creator-POV) gets its remaining videos switched on (027, or 031/034 for AS3, 032 for AS2). Pause the single worst ad in the laggard set. Do not touch anything under 3 days of data.
WEEK 3 - scale + retarget:

Duplicate the winning ad set at 2x budget. Engaged-but-didn't-buy goes to retargeting with the stack/what's-inside closer format (page-through of all 71 pages, bump wall preview). Hold 029 (mom's Bible) for retargeting too; guilt angles convert warmest on second touch.
FIELD KEY (for whoever builds the ads)
VERIFICATION & FLAGS

Copy = video, verified. Every primary text opens with the decoded on-screen hook block word for word (openings confirmed by re-OCR of the full hook zone on all 10, including v4 'your mom gave you a Bible', v5 'you want to believe', v6 'you've wanted to read the Bible your whole life', v7 'you lead the morning devotional', v8 'you've opened the Bible a hundred times', v9 'you've never known where to start', v10 'restarting itself now feels like proof'). Body lines trace to decoded VO beats, including v1's 'i thought i needed more discipline' and v9's 'quit isn't doubt', both recovered frame-exact. No invented scenes.

Decoded from the actual files. All 10 hooks, caption flows, formats, and real durations pulled via frame extraction + OCR of the burned-in text, plus waveform analysis of the audio. Durations 29–46s, inside the reference winner range (38–55s).

Sync model confirmed. VO energy present from t=0 on all 10 (speech-band audio measured in the first 1.2s of every file). POV hook blocks run second person; follow-up captions flip to first person on the same facts. The locked POV↔VO mechanic was followed, not the old hold-3s model.

Offer pill confirmed on 9 of 10 from frame one, each with its own subline: 71 Pages · Plain English (v1), illustrated · Book by book (v2), Everyday language (v3), Start here, then the Bible (v4), One section at a time (v5), All 66 books (v6), Parent-simple (v7), You'll actually finish it (v8), The restart that sticks (v10). Per-ad subline variation is a nice touch; log it in the DNA sheet as a testable variable.

FLAG - VIDEO 9 PILL: the pill is not reliably legible until ~0:15 (undetectable at 0:00–0:12 in testing, readable at 0:15+). Either it fades in late or the contrast fails against the bright background. The locked brief requires the pill pinned from frame one. Eyeball 0:00–0:12; if it's late or washed out, send back for re-export before launch. Video 7's pill is present from frame one but low-contrast over the forest B-roll in the opening seconds; acceptable, worth a stroke/scrim on the next revision.

CRITICAL FLAG - VIDEO 8, 0:07–0:11: OCR repeatedly reads stage-direction-like text in the caption track in this window: 'VOICEOVER', what appears to be 'OVERRUNS', and 'BLOCK … PAUSE'. That's the vocabulary of the editor brief, not ad copy. Manually eyeball 0:07–0:11 before this ad goes live. If brief text is burned in, reject 033 for re-export. If it turns out to be legitimate copy over the waterfall B-roll, clear the flag and launch as-is.

AUDIO FLAG - confirm by listening: waveforms confirm human-speech-band VO on all 10 from frame one, but files were decoded visually, not by ear. Confirm all 10 are real human female VO per the locked brief (no AI voice; Meta suppression roughly doubles CPMs). Names are written a:UGC voiceover. If any turn out AI-voiced, enforce the brief and re-record; do not just flip the tag.

Creator check: creators visually confirmed real-looking on v1 (bedroom, Bible in lap) and v2 (mom + toddler over an open Bible). v3 creator appears ~0:03–0:20. v4–v10 are faceless as briefed. Given the Batch 1 AI-creator findings, do one manual pass on v1–v3 faces before launch.
Compliance clean: no income or money claims anywhere (product outcome is understanding and relief), no em-dashes in copy, lowercase confessional tone throughout, testimonial framing only.

Cluster coverage this batch: AGAIN x3 (026, 031, 035), WTS x2 (031, 034), FEAR x2 (027, 030), LANG (028), WRONG (032), BORE/LD (033), DRIFT/GUILT (029). TIME is still uncovered white space; reserve it for the next batch as planned.
COMPANION CONTEXT

Hooks in this batch trace to the established cluster map (LANG, BORE, LD, FEAR, AGAIN, WTS, WRONG, DRIFT/GUILT). Ad copy above is written to mirror each video's on-screen hook and VO beats so the scroll-stop and the primary text tell one story.
